title: Spot the Silent Change (Synonymous SNP)
learning_goal: Learn that a Single Nucleotide Polymorphism (SNP) can change a codon's letters without changing the amino acid it codes for, because the genetic code is redundant (several codons can encode the same amino acid). This is called a synonymous (silent) mutation.
rules: The reference DNA sequence and reference protein are shown on screen the whole time — your sample may or may not match them.
Build the sample tiles first, then assign amino acids, before you're shown the comparison.
There is exactly ONE nucleotide difference between your sample and the reference.
A single nucleotide change does not always change the amino acid — check the protein comparison, not just the DNA, before deciding.
materials_provided: The reference (known/healthy) nucleotide sequence AND its reference amino acid sequence, both shown on screen the whole time
A restricted set of 48 nucleotide squares - one full tile set, but built from an unlabeled SAMPLE sequence that may or may not match the reference exactly
16 amino acid squares (including one '*' STOP tile) to assign to the sample sequence's codons
instructions: 1. Arrange the 48 nucleotide squares in order to build the sample sequence you were given (its identity - mutant or identical to reference - is not revealed yet).
2. Split into 16 codons (the last one is a STOP codon) and assign the matching amino acid tile under each codon using the codon table.
3. Compare your arranged sample sequence, position by position, against the reference cadF sequence shown at the top of the screen. Find the ONE nucleotide position that differs.
4. Compare your assigned amino acid sequence against the reference amino acid sequence (also shown at the top). Are they the same or different at the position of the SNP?
5. If the amino acid is unchanged, the game confirms: 'Synonymous mutation - the protein is unaffected!' and explains why using the codon table (show both codons map to the same letter).

mutation_details.change: T -> C at the 3rd (wobble) position of codon 9
mutation_details.classification: Synonymous (silent) SNP - both GTT and GTC code for Valine, so the protein sequence does not change.
